steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The phrase 'concern myself with' appears in the sentence 'I did not concern myself with those things' — referring to his sisters' dolls and giggling. The question asks for the closest meaning in context.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Buy': the narrator is describing his indifference to his sisters' activities, not a purchase. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Feel any anger toward': nothing in the passage suggests the narrator is angry — he is simply detached and uninterested. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Take any interest in': 'concern myself with' means to involve oneself in or pay attention to something. Saying he did not take any interest in those things captures exactly this detached stance. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Create': the narrator is not describing making anything — he is distancing himself from what his sisters do. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
